flags.concern: Effect migration: Step 3 changes the registry source before any backfill or dual-read path, so existing custom effects can disappear during rollout.
flags.evidence: The current editor bootstraps runtime effects only from `useEffects()` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx:18-22`, and `useEffects()` reads only `public.effects` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffects.ts:13-17`. The plan switches `useEffectRegistry` to resources in Step 3, but migration/back-compat is deferred to Step 10, so any existing `custom:*` clips lose their code source unless the rollout dual-reads or backfills first.

flags.concern: Effect identity: Public effects keyed only by `custom:slug` will collide across users, causing the wrong code to register, render, or reopen for editing.
flags.evidence: Dynamic effects are stored in the registry by normalized name only in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts:13-20,51-52`, clip rendering also strips `custom:` and looks up by bare slug in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:93-106`, and the UI writes clip references as `custom:${slug}` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:174-183`. The existing table only guarantees `unique (user_id, slug)` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/migrations/20260325090000_create_video_editor_tables.sql:42-54`, so once public effects from multiple users are merged, duplicate slugs become ambiguous.

flags.concern: Effect validation: The current Sucrase compiler never throws on bad effect code, so the planned validation/error UI cannot reliably detect generation failures or gate saving.
flags.evidence: `compileWithTransform()` catches all transform/runtime errors and returns a fallback React component instead of surfacing an error in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx:41-77`, and `compileEffectAsync()` just returns that component in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx:94-95`. The plan's Step 6 assumes client validation can 'show compilation errors' and disable save based on failure, but the current API contract does not expose failure as a detectable result.

flags.concern: Properties panel coverage: The plan updates `ClipPanel` but misses `BulkClipPanel`, leaving multi-select effect dropdowns and custom-effect editing on the old table/editor path.
flags.evidence: Single-clip effect dropdowns and editor live in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:119-187`, but the same effect controls are separately implemented for multi-select in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/BulkClipPanel.tsx:269-359`. The plan only calls out `ClipPanel`, so the repository would end up with split behavior unless bulk mode is intentionally excluded.
